prompt:
Ops escalation for the log ingest library (rs-logingest). Three field reports landed this week and I can't tell whether it's one bug or several — the crate is small (src/lib.rs, src/parse.rs, src/dedup.rs, src/sink.rs), please root-cause and fix all of it.

Report 1 — EU fleet daily totals are low and events are hours off. Agents that stamp an explicit offset (`+01:00`, `-04:00`) or `Z` are ingested at exactly the right UTC second. But a lot of our older agents write bare local timestamps with no offset, and those events are landing at the wrong UTC time — and flipping `default_offset_minutes` in the ingest config doesn't move them at all, which makes no sense, that setting exists precisely for these agents. Around midnight the events even end up attributed to the wrong day.

Report 2 — when the same alert text fires on several hosts in the same second, only one survives. Ops swears web-1 AND web-2 both logged `disk full` at 21:10:05Z; the pipeline keeps one and counts the other as a duplicate. Genuinely repeated lines from a single host SHOULD still collapse — that part works and must keep working.

Report 3 — the tail of every import vanishes. Output comes out in blocks, and whatever doesn't fill the last block is gone: a 7-record file with batch size 3 yields 6 records, a 5-record file with batch size 2 yields 4, and a 1-record smoke test with batch size 10 yields nothing at all. `accepted` says the records were kept, but they never appear in `records`.

Repro: `cargo test --offline`. tests/ingest.rs encodes all three reports as failing regressions (with precomputed UTC epochs), plus the healthy paths that currently pass and must not regress. Constraints: fix the library under src/ only — do not touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore; no public API changes; stdlib only. Acceptance: the whole suite green.
